channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Mon, 30 Mar 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence for Mon, 30 Mar 2026.

item.title: CISA remediation deadline today for critical F5 BIG-IP RCE flaw. 🛡️
item.description:
The window for patching a critical unauthenticated RCE in your access management infrastructure closes today. 🛡️
CVE-2025-53521 · Severity 9.8 · CISA KEV Remediation Deadline: March 30‚ 2026.
The @[CISA](urn:li:organization:13010360) has mandated that all federal agencies remediate a critical vulnerability in @[F5](urn:li:organization:1260) BIG-IP Access Policy Manager ❨APM❩ by the end of today. The flaw allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code with administrative privileges on the target system.
Because BIG-IP APM sits at the junction of corporate identity and network access‚ a compromise here provides attackers with a persistent foothold to bypass MFA and move laterally into the internal environment. Active exploitation has been confirmed in multiple sectors.
The uncomfortable truth: Your perimeter gatekeeper is currently your most volatile vulnerability if it has not been patched in the last 72 hours.
→ Update F5 BIG-IP to the latest patched release immediately to close CVE-2025-53521. → Strictly isolate all management plane interfaces from the public internet using OOB networks. → Audit your APM logs for unauthorized administrative accounts or anomalous process executions.
#Cybersecurity #NetworkSecurity #F5 #PatchManagement #CISO #CodeDefence

item.title: Citrix NetScaler confirmed under exploitation in new global spree. 🛑
item.description:
Researchers have confirmed active exploitation of a memory overread flaw that could bypass all session security. 🛑
CVE-2026-3055 · Severity 9.3 · Active Exploitation in NetScaler ADC & Gateway.
Security researchers at watchTowr have confirmed that attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-3055 in @[Citrix](urn:li:organization:1598) NetScaler appliances. The flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to leak sensitive memory contents‚ including session tokens and credentials.
This campaign echoes the 2023 CitrixBleed attacks and is observed to be targeting large-scale enterprise deployments. If exploited‚ an attacker can hijack active sessions and bypass multi-factor authentication to gain full access to internal corporate resources.
The uncomfortable truth: Your browser-based remote access gateways are the primary targets for attackers seeking silent and persistent access to your enterprise core.
→ Update NetScaler ADC and Gateway to the latest security releases immediately. → Monitor for unusual session activity originating from non-standard geographic IP blocks. → Terminate all active sessions and force a re-authentication once the patches are applied.
#Cybersecurity #NetScaler #Citrix #SessionHijacking #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: European Commission confirms 350 GB data theft in AWS cloud hack. 🐚
item.description:
A threat actor has exfiltrated hundreds of gigabytes of data from the heart of EU executive cloud operations. 🐚
Data Breach Alert · European Commission AWS infrastructure compromised via credential theft.
The European Commission confirmed today that its @[Amazon Web Services ❨AWS❩](urn:li:organization:1598) environment was breached on March 24‚ 2026. The attacker claims to have exfiltrated over 350 GB of data‚ including internal databases and employee directories. Screenshots provided as proof show access to an internal employee email server.
While the Commission stated that its internal systems were not affected‚ the breach of public-facing cloud infrastructure highlights the risks of managing large-scale cloud estates for high-profile government bodies. AWS confirmed the hack resulted from compromised account credentials.
The uncomfortable truth: Your cloud security is only as strong as the credentials used to manage it; a single compromised admin account can expose terabytes of sovereign data.
→ Conduct a deep audit of all public-facing cloud credentials and administrative access keys. → Enforce strict phishing-resistant MFA for all cloud management accounts and service principals. → Implement enhanced egress filtering to detect large-scale data exfiltration from cloud storage buckets.
#Cybersecurity #CloudSecurity #EU #DataBreach #AWS #CodeDefence

item.title: CanisterWorm wiper attack targets cloud clusters in Iran timezone. 🐛
item.description:
A destructive new worm is systematically wiping data on cloud infrastructure targeting specific geographic regions. 🐛
Threat Alert · CanisterWorm targeting exposed Docker and Kubernetes clusters in Iran.
Security researchers have identified a destructive variant of the CanisterWorm malware. Attributed to the group TeamPCP‚ the worm spreads via exposed cloud APIs and includes a wiper component that triggers if the infected system uses the Iran timezone or has Farsi set as the default language.
The malware targets @[Docker](urn:li:organization:3071271) and Kubernetes instances‚ destroying all stored data on infected nodes. This represents a significant escalation in the industrialization of geopolitical cyberattacks targeting cloud-native infrastructure.
The uncomfortable truth: Your cloud-native control planes are now front-line targets for automated‚ destructive operations that can wipe your entire production environment in seconds.
→ Audit all Docker and Kubernetes APIs to ensure they are not exposed to the public internet. → Rotate all cloud provider credentials and CI/CD tokens that may have been exposed in recent scans. → Implement strict network isolation for container management planes to prevent automated worm propagation.
#Cybersecurity #CloudSecurity #CanisterWorm #WiperMalware #Docker #CodeDefence
